rulings.Case Title: NATALIE LUCAS et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 13, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT EDWARD S. WALSH CO. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment is denied. Triable issue re exposure. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN GIDDING et al VS. IVAN KAYE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 13, 2009, line 2, DEFENDANT TOM CYGNAR MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Both motions continued to November 19 on the court¿s own motion. Moving party on the motion for judgment on the pleadings to provide courtesy copy of the moving papers to department 302 no later than the end of the day November 13.=(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN GIDDING et al VS. IVAN KAYE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 13, 2009, line 2, DEFENDANTS IVAN KAYE, TOM SMITH, AND, BSI SERVICES PTY LTD Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings. Both motions continued to November 19 on the court¿s own motion. Moving party on the motion for judgment on the pleadings to provide courtesy copy of the moving papers to department 302 no later than the end of the day November 13. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL RATNER et al VS. 1314 POLK STREET ASSOCIATES, LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 13, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT THE CHRIS JAMES CORPORATION DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer is overruled with ten days to answer. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL RATNER et al VS. 1314 POLK STREET ASSOCIATES, LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 13, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT THE CHRIS JAMES CORPORATION MOTION TO STRIKE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Motion to strike is denied. The complaint alleges sufficient facts to state claims for fraud, negligent misrepresentation and restitution. Although the last claim is improperly labeled unjust enrichment, the label is of no consequence. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID WONG et al VS. MARINA BICHINSKY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 13, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT MARINA BICHINSKY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for summary adjudication is denied. The motion fails to comply with CRC 3.1350 regarding the separate statement. Defendant has failed to sustain her burden of production as to absence of special damages. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN ENG VS. UNITED COMMERCIAL BANK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Complaint Supporting Declaration Memorandum Of P/A
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 13, 2009, line 11, PLAINTIFF MARTIN ENG Motion To Amend Complaint. Motion for leave to amend is granted so that, if he wishes, plaintiff may within ten days add a new claim for breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing and/or unfair business practices, but not a claim for breach of fiduciary duty. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANITA BARNES VS. LESLIE BARHAM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Reasonable Attorneys' Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 13, 2009, line 5, PLAINTIFF ANITA BARNES Motion For Reasonable Attorneys' Fees and Costs. Continued to December 2, 2009 on the court¿s own motion, so the motion could be heard by Judge Woolard. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID C SOWARD VS. CAPITOL CITY ESCROW, INC., A CALIFORNIA
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 13, 2009, line 6, DEFENDANT CAPITOL CITY ESCROW, INC., DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Continued to December 8, 2009 per the agreement of the parties. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-09-492725 WITH CASE CGC-09-492524
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 13, 2009, line 7, APPELLANT FERAT INC. MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-09-492725 WITH CASE CGC-09-492524. Continued to November 25, 2009 per the ex parte order. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY VS. JAMAL BUTLER et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 13, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT JAMAL BUTLER DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. San Francisco Housing Authority. Demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give the plaintiff an opportunity, if it is able to do so, to allege facts regarding likely continuing conduct and why criminal prosecution is not adequate. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Dismiss Plaintiffs Complaint Against American Biltrite Inc. For Failure To Prosecute
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 13, 2009, line 9, DEFENDANT AMERICAN BILTRITE INC. MOTION To Dismiss Plaintiffs Complaint Against American Biltrite Inc. For Failure To Prosecute. Motion to dismiss is denied on grounds of insufficient notice. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CAPURRO PROPERTIES LLC VS. GENE BAKER
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 13, 2009, line 10, PLAINTIFF CAPURRO PROPERTIES LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Triable issues regarding habitability defense, validity of amount demanded in the three day notice, and validity of waiver regarding the sixty day notice. =(302/HEK)
